excerpt: It is by virtue of this consummation of certainty that ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib surpassed countless prophets in rank and spiritual station.
source_work: al-Aqwal al-Dhahabiyya (The Golden Sayings) or Rahat al-ʿAql (The Repose of the Intellect)
source_location_hint: Discussions of the three degrees of certainty and Abraham's spiritual elevation

excerpt: Yet the Messenger of God, Muhammad, declared: Had Jesus possessed a yet greater measure of perfected certainty, he would have walked upon the air itself.
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama (The Lamps in Proving Imāmate)
source_location_hint: Discussions of prophetic and apostolic grades of certainty

excerpt: even if all the veils and curtains of existence were stripped away from before my eyes, my certainty would not increase even by a single degree
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql (The Repose of the Intellect) and related Kirmani works
source_location_hint: Transmitted sayings of ʿAlī on the perfection of certainty

excerpt: The Prophet Solomon was my student, and I myself taught him the languages of all creatures—jinn and beast, bird and dragon.
source_work: al-Riyad (The Gardens) and Kitab al-Yanabiʿ (The Springs)
source_location_hint: Narratives concerning ʿAlī's knowledge and dominion over creation

excerpt: When I raised my gaze toward the heaven, my sight penetrated through all the veils of the upper throne and beyond. When I directed my sight earthward, my vision tore through all the layers of the earth itself.
source_work: al-Riyad (The Gardens) and al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_location_hint: Narratives of Gabriel's appearance in ʿAlī's court and the Commander's knowledge of all realms

excerpt: The wall of sand transformed before all eyes into shimmering gold...Such is the station of one whom the Creator has clothed with authority over all creation.
source_work: al-Riyad (The Gardens) and Tanbih al-Hadi (The Guidance of the Seeker)
source_location_hint: Miracles of ʿAlī demonstrating dominion over creation

excerpt: I have been given the complete knowledge of the Book. I am God's command, God's wisdom, God's cause, and God's light in the heavens and the earth.
source_location_hint: Sayings of ʿAlī on his station and knowledge

excerpt: Rise up, O Madrik ibn ʿAnzala ibn Salāma, by the command of God and His proof upon earth! In that instant, the dead man arose and sat upright.
source_work: Kitāb al-Muʿajizāt al-Kulliyya (The Book of Universal Miracles) and al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_location_hint: Narratives of resurrection and testimony of the dead through ʿAlī's authority

excerpt: The Messenger of God descended, surrounded by the luminous angels...The Prophet then ascended back into the veils of light, accompanied by the angels.
source_location_hint: Narratives of the Prophet's return in light and ʿAlī's supreme station

excerpt: This man came upon me and struck my arm with his sword with such force that it was severed from my body. From that day to this, I have been gripped with fear whenever I perceive his presence.
source_work: al-Riyad (The Gardens) and Kitab al-Yanabiʿ (The Springs)
source_location_hint: Narratives of ʿAlī's protection of the prophets in past ages

excerpt: I slew them by piercing and splitting their forms asunder...I read unto them the verses of the Qurʾān, and by the power of God's word, they were destroyed...I clothed them in human form and struck them down with this sword.
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama (The Lamps in Proving Imāmate) and Tanbih al-Hadi
source_location_hint: Accounts of ʿAlī's battles with jinn and forces of evil

excerpt: He placed it in Salmān's trembling hands...ʿAlī had read the hidden thought in Salmān's heart—a subtle arrogance that had crept into his soul—and he had removed it with perfect wisdom.
source_location_hint: Narratives of ʿAlī's knowledge of the unseen and his relationship with the companions

excerpt: If the staff of Moses swallowed the false serpents and then preserved them whole within itself, how much more shall this lion preserve the magician alive within its belly?
source_work: al-Riyad (The Gardens) and al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_location_hint: Miracles of Imāms and their authority over creation

excerpt: The Imām had thus revealed to the man, through three successive states, the reality of his absolute authority and dominion over his own being. The flesh, the corruption, the very substance of the body itself—all obey the will of the Imām.
source_location_hint: Miracles of the Imāms demonstrating divine authority

excerpt: Thou didst remember me with sincerity, and so I came to thee. Thus does the Imām of each age stand as the guardian of God's truth.
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama (The Lamps in Proving Imāmate) and Tanbih al-Hadi
source_location_hint: Narratives of the Imām's presence and protection of the faithful
